title: Celebrating Veterans Day, and every day, with IHG® Army Hotels
description: We’re saying thanks to our veterans through these travel tips, special rates and sweepstakes.
text:
“Thank you for your service!”
It’s the phrase you may hear a lot on Veterans Day, though hopefully you hear it all year long.
Service, as it is for you, is at the core of everything we do at IHG® Army Hotels, and we’re proud to have veterans and military spouses on our team. They’re uniquely qualified to provide superior service to our guests, who are often veterans themselves. In the spirit of honoring our heroes this year, take a look at how we’re saying thanks to our veterans through these travel tips, special rates and an exciting sweepstakes.
Enter for a Chance to Win Huge Rewards
We’re giving away a total of 1 million IHG® One Rewards points in our annual Veterans Day sweepstakes. Ten lucky winners will each receive 100,000 points—that’s enough points for 3-night stay in most of our off-post IHG Hotels and Resorts across the globe. To explore all of your options for your next trip, visit the IHG Locations page. We’ll be posting more information on our Instagram and Facebook pages, so head over there to stay up to date.
Relax With Special Rates
We’re proud to offer our Retiree and Veteran rate all year long to those who have given so much of themselves through service. Military retirees, their spouses and military veterans can save up to 20% off their stays at any of our on-post locations nationwide.
Reservations can be made up to a year in advance, and we have several properties near popular activities to help you get the most of your leisure time.
Golfing
Fort Gordon
Gordon Lakes Golf Club has one of the finest courses in the South. In fact, the original 18 holes were designed by renowned golf architect Robert Trent Jones Senior.
Stay on post at Holiday Inn Express® Griffith Hall or Candlewood Suites® Building 38300.
Fort Jackson
Golf all week long if you want! Fort Jackson Golf Club is now open 7 days a week. It also features Bogey’s Bar and Grill for when you want to kick back after hitting the links.
Stay on post at Holiday Inn Express Fort Jackson Inn or Candlewood Suites Building 10050.
Fort Bragg
Decisions. Decisions! Fort Bragg boasts two beautiful golf courses—Ryder Golf Course and Stryker Golf Course. With several water holes, Ryder is challenging for all skill levels while Stryker features a large club house, new locker rooms, and a well-stocked pro shop.
Stay on post at IHG Army Hotels Moon Hall or IHG Army Hotels Normandy House and Carolina Inn.
Hunting
Fort Huachuca
If you like your recreation a bit more rugged, head down to Arizona for deer hunting at the base of the Huachuca Mountains. The season runs through the end of December.
Stay on post at IHG Army Hotels Building 43083 or Candlewood Suites Building 44420.
Fort Bragg
When you’re done chasing birdies on Fort Bragg’s golf courses, you can also take on some deer. Hunting season runs through January 2.
Stay on post at IHG Army Hotels Moon Hall or IHG Army Hotels Normandy House and Carolina Inn.
Fishing
Fort Drum
What’s more relaxing than fishing? Fort Drum offers many beautiful places to fish for brook, brown and rainbow trout. Plus, Veterans Day is listed as a Free Fishing Day, so no fishing license is required.
Stay on post at Candlewood Suites Building 9090.
Fort Riley
Try your skill (or luck) at nearly 30 managed fishing ponds for rainbow trout or channel catfish. Also be sure to take one of the many scenic views.
Stay on post at Candlewood Suites Building 6956.
With hotel locations on 40 posts across the US, we have a wide variety of destinations for retirees, veterans and their families to enjoy at a reduced rate.
We hope you take advantage of the special discounts and perks that many companies offer on Veterans Day. Above all, we hope you know how much your service means to us every day.
